etymology.description: Depicts a footprint pointing up, representing 'foot' -> stylized as 龰
etymology.interpretation: Originally depicted as a footprint with toes and a sole, 龰(龰) (yù, 'stylized feet') serves as a stylized version of the footprint, maintaining the concept of the foot. Over time, similar characters evolved, like 趾(趾) (zhǐ, 'toe'), which uses a similar base pictographic representation.
